methods.name: Implant (Jadelle/Implanon)
benefits: 99.9% effective - one of the most effective methods
Lasts 3-5 years depending on the brand
Reversible - fertility returns immediately after removal
Safe for most women including those who can't take estrogen
side_effects: Irregular bleeding or spotting (most common, especially first 3-6 months)
Weight changes (minimal evidence for significant gain)
Mood changes (monitor; consider removal if severe)
methods.side_effect_management: Irregular bleeding often improves by 6 months. If bothersome, a course of ibuprofen or combined pills (if no contraindications) may help. For persistent severe side effects, removal is always an option.
methods.who_should_use: Women who want long-term, highly effective contraception without daily action. Good for women who cannot use estrogen. Suitable for all ages including adolescents.
methods.who_should_not_use: Very few contraindications. Not recommended for women with current breast cancer or severe liver disease.

methods.name: Copper IUD
benefits: No hormones - no hormonal side effects
Reversible - fertility returns immediately after removal
Can be used as emergency contraception up to 5 days after unprotected sex
side_effects: Heavier and longer periods (especially first 3-6 months)
methods.side_effect_management: Heavy bleeding often improves after 6 months. NSAIDs (ibuprofen) can help with cramping. If severe bleeding persists, removal is an option.
methods.who_should_use: Women who want long-term, hormone-free contraception. Good for women who cannot use hormones. Suitable for all ages.
methods.who_should_not_use: Current pelvic infection, known copper allergy, Wilson's disease, unexplained vaginal bleeding, uterine abnormalities.

methods.name: Hormonal IUD (Mirena/Kyleena)
benefits: Lighter periods - many women stop having periods entirely
side_effects: Hormonal side effects (less common than pills due to local action)
Ovarian cysts (usually harmless and resolve on their own)
methods.side_effect_management: Irregular bleeding typically improves by 6 months. Ibuprofen helps with cramping. Most women report high satisfaction after the adjustment period.
methods.who_should_use: Women who want long-term contraception AND lighter periods. Good for women with heavy periods or painful periods.
methods.who_should_not_use: Current pelvic infection, unexplained vaginal bleeding, uterine abnormalities, current breast cancer, severe liver disease.

methods.name: Injectable (Depo-Provera)
benefits: Private - no one needs to know you're using it
side_effects: Weight gain (average 2-5 lbs over first year)
Delayed return to fertility (6-12 months after last shot)
Decreased bone density with long-term use (reversible after stopping)
methods.side_effect_management: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation recommended for long-term users. Weight can be managed with diet and exercise. Return to fertility is delayed but not permanent.
methods.who_should_use: Women who want a method they don't have to think about daily. Good for women who can't use estrogen.
methods.who_should_not_use: Current breast cancer, unexplained vaginal bleeding, severe liver disease, osteoporosis risk factors for long-term use.

methods.name: Combined Oral Contraceptive Pill
benefits: Reduces risk of ovarian and endometrial cancer
side_effects: Nausea (improves with time - take with food)
Small increased risk of blood clots (very low for healthy non-smokers under 35)
methods.side_effect_management: Take with food to reduce nausea. Most side effects resolve within 3 months. If not, try a different pill formulation.
methods.who_should_use: Healthy non-smoking women under 35. Women who want cycle control and non-contraceptive benefits.
methods.who_should_not_use: Smokers over 35, migraine with aura, uncontrolled hypertension, history of blood clots, current breast cancer, severe liver disease.

methods.name: Progestin-Only Pill (Mini-Pill)
benefits: Safe for women who can't take estrogen
Safe for women with migraines (even with aura)
Safe for smokers of any age
methods.side_effect_management: Must be taken at the same time every day (+/- 3 hours window). Irregular bleeding often improves with time.
methods.who_should_use: Women who cannot take estrogen - smokers over 35, migraine with aura, hypertension, breastfeeding. Good alternative to combined pill.
methods.who_should_not_use: Current breast cancer, severe liver disease.

methods.name: Male Condom
benefits: 85% effective with typical use (98% with perfect use)
methods.side_effects: No medical side effects. Some people report reduced sensation or latex allergy (non-latex options available).
methods.side_effect_management: Use with water-based lubricant to improve comfort. Try different brands/sizes for better fit.
methods.who_should_use: Anyone - especially those at risk of STIs. Great as backup method or primary method when used consistently.
methods.who_should_not_use: Latex allergy (use polyurethane or polyisoprene condoms instead).
